pattern: Two strings are one swap apart exactly when they differ in zero positions or in precisely two positions that are mirror images of each other.
hints: Collect the indices where the two strings disagree.
Zero mismatches means they are already equal; any count other than zero or two cannot be fixed by a single swap.
If there are exactly two mismatch indices i and j, the swap works only when s1[i]==s2[j] and s1[j]==s2[i].

edge_inputs.description: swap of two mismatched positions makes them equal
more than two differences cannot be fixed
exactly one mismatch cannot be fixed by one swap
two mismatches but not a valid swap pair

stress.description: random equal-length strings over a tiny alphabet so swaps often nearly work
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    alpha = 'abc'
    s1 = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(n))
    s2 = list(s1)
    flips = rng.randrange(0, 4)
    for _ in range(flips):
        idx = rng.randrange(n)
        s2[idx] = rng.choice(alpha)
    return [s1, ''.join(s2)]
